title: MOER/OER — Shared Definitions
description: Reusable JSON Schema definitions shared across NYC Mayor's Office of Environmental Remediation (MOER/OER) object schemas. Reconciles fields common to OER's Open Data datasets (data.cityofnewyork.us — notably 'OER Cleanup Sites' 3279-pp7v), nyc.gov/site/oer, the public SPEED map (Searchable Property Environmental E-Designation, speed.cityofnewyork.us, a CARTO/Leaflet app), and the login-walled EPIC project portal (a002-epic.nyc.gov, an OER-owned ASP.NET/AngularJS system). OER data is strongly parcel/BBL-keyed, so the geography spine and BBL/BIN identifiers are $ref'd from nyc-commons. Note: the authoritative (E)-designation inventory is published under the Department of City Planning (DCP) label, not OER — OER administers resolving the (E) requirement.

x-nyc-commons.note: Geography/identifier/address/money definitions are $ref'd from the canonical nyc-commons set; agency-specific definitions remain local. OER records are BBL/BIN-keyed, so nyc-commons BBL is leaned on heavily.

Borough.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/Borough). Back-compatible: the $defs name is unchanged, so object schemas referencing _common.json#/$defs/Borough still resolve.

Geography.$comment: Canonical definition provided by nyc-commons (geography.json#/$defs/GeographySpine). OER's 'OER Cleanup Sites' dataset (3279-pp7v) carries a full spine — BBL, BIN, Borough, Community Board, Council District, Census Tract, NTA, Postcode — mapped here.

BBL.$comment: Canonical BBL from nyc-commons. OER's core join key: nearly every OER record (cleanup site, e-designation, historic land use, clean soil bank) is keyed to a tax lot (BBL).

BIN.$comment: Canonical BIN from nyc-commons; carried on OER Cleanup Sites.

TaxLot.description: NYC Borough-Block-Lot identity carried by nearly every OER record — the join key back to PLUTO/MapPLUTO (DCP) and the rest of city government. OER is a parcel-centric agency: a 'site' is a lot.

OERProgram.description: Which OER remedial program a site is enrolled in ('OER Program'). The Voluntary Cleanup Program (VCP) and the E-Designation/Restrictive Declaration Program are OER's two land-cleanup programs.

EType.description: The category of environmental requirement carried by an (E)-designation or Restrictive Declaration. OER manages three: hazardous materials, noise, and air.

RemedialPhase.description: Where a site sits in OER's documented remedial process ('Phase'), from pre-application through Notice of Satisfaction. Mirrors the OER Remedial Process: Application/Investigation → Remedial Plan Development → Notice To Proceed/Decision Document → Remedial Action Implementation → Remedial Closure Report → Notice Of Satisfaction/Completion.

SiteClass.description: OER site class ('Class') — OER's tracking classification for an enrolled cleanup site.

PropertyReference.description: A reference to an OER-tracked property by its identifiers.

oerProjectNumber.description: OER Project Number ('OER Project Numbers') — OER's primary project key.

PartyContact.description: An owner / developer / applicant contact on an OER matter.
